Given the anatomical distribution mentioned in some of the references like "procedures on nose, mouth and pharynx" being part of the Excision method, and considering typical presentations of similar pediatric tumors, maxillary bones would likely host many soft-tissue swellings due to their prominence and proximity to mucosal surfaces.
